query: Has Axios been updated to correctly handle loopback addresses with trailing dots when checking proxy rules?
Has the issue with unsanitized company info fields in system settings being rendered on public pages without proper encoding been fixed?
Can code execution as root be triggered via the public webhook endpoint without authentication?
Has the issue with the /skServer/enableSecurity endpoint remaining open after initial setup been fixed?
Has a fix been released for the issue where the REST connector can make unauthorized requests to internal systems due to an empty default blacklist?
Has the issue where attackers can run arbitrary Python code by manipulating a parameter in the model execution component been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue with user input in the name and surname fields being executed as JavaScript in the backend users page been fixed?
Has the issue with malicious JavaScript code being injected through blog category titles and executed on public pages been fixed?
Has the issue with malicious JavaScript code being injected into blog posts and executed when viewed been fixed?
Has the issue with unsanitized blog post category content allowing malicious JavaScript injection been fixed?
Has the issue with unsanitized input in page creation and editing being rendered without encoding been fixed?
Has the issue with user-inputted post data being rendered unsafely in menus been fixed?
Has the issue with user-added pages in menus executing scripts without proper sanitization been fixed?
Has the issue with malicious JavaScript code being injected through backup filenames and executed when viewing backups been fixed?
Has the issue where user input in error logs is rendered unsafely and can execute scripts when admins view the logs page been fixed?
Has the issue with unsanitized blog tag names allowing malicious JavaScript injection been fixed?
Can an unauthenticated attacker still perform actions on behalf of a user who initiates a password reset in our current Payload version?
Can visiting a malicious website still inject JavaScript code into SiYuan's Electron Node.js context without user interaction?
Has the issue with malicious URLs in Attribute View fields executing arbitrary OS commands when opened in Gallery or Kanban view been fixed?
Has the issue with path parameters being substituted into URLs without encoding been fixed to prevent directory traversal attacks?
Has the issue where user-supplied JavaScript can escape the sandbox and execute code as root been fixed?
Has the issue with the backup restore mechanism allowing tampering and injection of malicious configuration been fixed?
Has the issue where Handlebars directly injects unquoted numeric literal values into generated JavaScript been fixed?
Has the issue where user input can be used to execute arbitrary code in SimpleVectorStore been fixed in the latest Spring AI version?
Can a user with write access to a project still escalate to admin by moving it under a project they own?
Has the issue of Helm installing plugins without verifying their signatures been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue where a specially crafted Helm plugin can overwrite arbitrary files on the system been fixed in the latest version?
Can the OIDC callback still issue a full JWT token without checking if a matched user has two-factor authentication enabled?
Has the issue where the ARC broadcaster mistakenly treats certain failed transactions as successful been fixed?
Has a fix been released for the FTP command injection issue via CRLF sequences in file paths?
Has the issue with the safe_join function allowing directory traversal to access arbitrary files been fixed in the latest version?
Has a fix been released to prevent authenticated users from tricking the server into making unauthorized HTTP requests to arbitrary URLs?
Has the issue where attackers can manipulate topic names to bypass authorization and intercept private updates been fixed?
Can commands like "kenv" be hijacked by manipulating the system's PATH environment variable after the recent security updates?
Can code injection into the .env file via unvalidated host parameters in the install controller still occur?
Can an attacker still access the setup wizard when the database is unreachable, allowing them to overwrite the .env file?
Has the issue with executing commands due to insufficient validation of user-supplied Stata do-file content been fixed in the latest version of stata-mcp?
Can anyone still access the cache server without authentication and read replicated resources?
Has the issue where a symlink in a partials or layouts directory can load files outside those directories been fixed?
Has the issue where an attacker can access the MCP server through a victim's browser been fixed in the latest java-sdk version?
Has the issue where multiple baggage header lines can cause high CPU and memory usage been fixed?
Has the issue where denied files can still be returned to the browser been fixed in the latest Vite dev server update?
Can Vite dev server's WebSocket still expose arbitrary server files via a custom event without a valid Origin header?
Has the issue of malicious VSIX files writing arbitrary files outside the extension directory been fixed in coder/code-marketplace?
Can user input with a __proto__ key still override default values when passed to the defu function?
Can users still overwrite others' files by manipulating the filename parameter in the file management API?
Can uploading a plugin with a filename containing ../ still allow deleting arbitrary directories and writing files to any path?
Has the issue of passing user-supplied URLs to Android's intent system without validation been fixed in mobile-mcp?
Can a token with only "projects.background" permission still delete a project background?
Can files larger than the maximum size limit be uploaded by manipulating the file size in the JSON metadata of the import zip?
Has the issue with user-controlled task titles breaking iCalendar property boundaries through CRLF characters been fixed?
Has the issue with task titles in overdue emails creating phishing links due to unescaped Markdown characters been fixed?
Has the issue with the repeating task function consuming excessive CPU by looping billions of times when given a short interval and past due date been fixed?
Can any authenticated CalDAV user still read task data from other projects by guessing or knowing a task's UID?
Has the issue with the TOTP lockout mechanism not persisting due to a database rollback been fixed?
Can authenticated users still read labels they shouldn't have access to due to a SQL operator precedence issue in the access control query?
Has the issue where Helm writes chart contents to the wrong directory when using "helm pull --untar" been fixed in the latest version?
Do JWT tokens for deleted or downgraded link shares still remain valid for 72 hours after changes are made?
Has the issue where screenshots can be accessed without authorization been fixed in the latest software update?
Has the issue with valid authentication requests failing 50% of the time due to certain RegExp modifiers been fixed?
Can a user still soft-delete synced non-manual transactions through the update endpoint, bypassing the normal deletion protection?
Has the issue where the OTLP HTTP exporter reads the full HTTP response into memory without a size limit, potentially causing memory exhaustion, been fixed?
Can pages still be edited to inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in visitors' browsers due to missing HTML sanitization?
Can an admin still inject JavaScript code into the user management page by adding malicious input to the blacklist note field?
Can HTML-entity-encoded JavaScript injected via an iframe's srcdoc attribute still execute in the parent page's context after the latest updates?
Can users with limited file editor permissions still access and modify sensitive files like .env and composer.json through the API?
Can a malicious server still crash the client through concurrent session closures in the latest version of Axios?
Has the issue where renderFile can access files outside the specified root directory been fixed in a newer version of liquidjs?
Can the sort_natural filter still bypass security options and expose sensitive prototype properties through a sorting side-channel attack?
Can authentication be bypassed on protected webapi routes by forging an X-lobe-chat-auth header using the hardcoded XOR key?
Has the issue with upload filename sanitization allowing arbitrary file writes on Windows using backslashes been fixed?
Has the issue where an attacker can impersonate a trusted TimeStamping Authority by appending a spoofed certificate been fixed?
Has the issue with cookie name normalization allowing attackers to override legitimate cookies with specially crafted names been fixed?
Has the issue with IPv4 rules not matching IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses been fixed?
Can files still be written outside the output directory using specially crafted dynamic route parameters during static site generation?
Has the issue with serveStatic allowing access to protected files using repeated slashes in the request path been fixed?
Can using repeated slashes in a request path still bypass authorization middleware and access protected static files?
Can unauthorized users still access task metadata without logging in through the task polling endpoints?
Can authenticated users still access any system's containers by guessing or enumerating the system and container IDs?
Has the issue with Wasmtime's pooling allocator leaking linear memory contents between instances been fixed?
Has the issue with cloning a wasmtime Linker causing a use-after-free bug and potential segfault been fixed?
Has the issue with Wasmtime's Winch compiler disclosing host stack data to WebAssembly guests due to incorrect table size handling been fixed?
Has the issue where reading a corrupted image from the clipboard crashes the app been fixed in our current software version?
Has the issue with the unpacker using uninitialized memory from a scratch buffer been fixed in the latest version of openexr?
Can files still be uploaded with a mismatched Content-Type header that differs from their filename extension?
Has the issue with offscreen rendering causing a crash or memory corruption when a released texture is accessed after its native state is freed been fixed in our current version?
Has the issue with unsanitized IMAP search commands allowing mail search injection or CSRF bypass been fixed?
Has the issue with unauthenticated attackers being able to write files arbitrarily via crafted session data been fixed in the latest Roundcube Webmail version?
Can a user still exploit the JSON-patch endpoint to read internal Node functions by targeting the prototype with a crafted "from" property?
Has the issue where the app's executable path is written to the Run registry key without quoting when setting login item settings been fixed?
Can a NetworkManager still modify a subscriber's policy using a different IMSI in the request body than in the URL path?
Has the issue with the C parser accepting null bytes and control characters in response headers been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue where an attacker can inject extra headers by manipulating the reason parameter in a response been fixed?
Does the latest version of aiohttp still drop the Authorization header but retain sensitive Cookie and Proxy-Authorization headers when following redirects to a different origin?
Can the gateway still fetch internal URLs through the image pipeline after updating to the latest version?
Has the issue with go-git's index decoder causing a runtime panic when reading a maliciously crafted index file been fixed?
Can an attacker create multiple sessions using a single MFA recovery code by sending concurrent login requests?
Can authenticated users still read others' private memories through the collection query API?
Has the issue where empty allowlists are treated as unset and silently undo deny-all revocations been fixed in our current version?
Can files still be written outside the config directory by manipulating zip entry names to bypass sanitization and achieve remote code execution?
Has the issue with improper validation been fixed in our current software version?
Can an attacker reuse a single MFA recovery code multiple times by sending concurrent login requests with a user's password?
Can a low-privileged user still access private asset editor metadata without authorization by calling the image-editor endpoint with a restricted asset ID?
Can anonymous users still fetch transformed image bytes from private assets without authentication?
Has the issue with the Content Security Policy template being modified to allow unsafe scripts been fixed?
Has the inefficient regular expression complexity issue in AdlLexer been fixed in the latest pygments version?
Has the issue where middleware runs on unrelated routes due to a simple path prefix check been fixed?
Has the issue where authenticated users can bypass key-level authorization using nested transactions been fixed in the latest etcd version?
Has a fix been released for the issue where Server-Sent Events can cause stream corruption in Spring MVC and WebFlux applications?
Has the issue where Wasm guest code can access host memory outside its sandbox using the Winch compiler been fixed?
Has the issue with Wasmtime's Cranelift backend allowing guest modules to bypass bounds checks and access arbitrary host memory been fixed?
Has the issue where unauthenticated users can bypass access controls and modify documents in Puck-registered collections been fixed?
Has the issue with user inputs being directly interpolated into shell commands in GitHub Actions workflows been fixed?
Has the issue where custom cache keys can cause valid tokens to return claims from other tokens been fixed?
Has the issue with Kedro's logging config allowing arbitrary system command execution during startup been fixed in our current version?
Can leading whitespace in a key string still bypass the public key validation and enable a JWT algorithm confusion attack?
Has the issue with malicious JavaScript code being stored and executed when administrators view group and role settings been fixed?
Has the issue with unsanitized JavaScript input in Methods Management fields being executed across all pages been fixed?
Can a user with pairing privileges but not admin privileges still approve device requests for broader scopes, including admin access?
Has the issue with shell commands being executed without sanitization when serving models with enable_mlserver=True been fixed?
Can making an unauthenticated POST request to the `/loadIG` endpoint still allow an attacker to steal authentication tokens by redirecting to a malicious URL?
Has the issue where MLflow executes arbitrary commands from a model's python_env.yaml file without sanitization been fixed in our current version?
Has the issue of tar.gz files overwriting arbitrary files due to lack of path validation during extraction been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue of user-controlled input being interpreted as raw SQL query fragments in MikroORM been fixed?
Can an attacker still overwrite arbitrary host files by manipulating systemd credential names to escape the container's credentials directory?
Can templates still read and write files anywhere on the host server with root privileges?
Has the issue with unverified certifier signatures being persisted when acquiring a certificate been fixed in the current version?
Has the issue with Drizzle ORM allowing SQL injection by not escaping identifier delimiters been fixed?
Has the issue with shell commands being constructed without proper sanitization of configuration values been fixed?
Has the issue of auto-created proxy-auth users inheriting execution rights from global defaults been fixed?
Has the issue where revoked user permissions don't restrict access to existing public share links been fixed?
Has the issue with user-supplied scope patterns causing workers to hang indefinitely due to infinite backtracking been fixed?
Has a fix been released to prevent managed cluster admins from forging client certificates to escalate privileges across clusters?
Has the issue where the OAuth state value exposes the PKCE verifier in the redirect URL been fixed in the current version?
Can tokens still be forged using the default secret if the TOKEN_SECRET environment variable is not set?
Has the issue with aggregate functions returning unmasked values for concealed fields, like API tokens, been fixed?
Can users with basic file upload permissions still overwrite any existing file by UUID through the TUS resumable upload endpoint?
Has the issue where IPv4-Mapped IPv6 addresses can bypass the private network request block been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue with the login page allowing malicious windows to intercept OAuth authorization flows been fixed?
Has the issue with arbitrary Python code execution from user-provided templates during containerization been fixed?
Has the issue of command injection via unquoted system packages in cloud deployment scripts been fully resolved?
Can a developer with create permission in one namespace still exfiltrate secrets from other namespaces using a single kubectl apply?
Has the issue with Wasmtime's table.grow operator causing incorrect typing and potential out-of-bounds memory access been fixed?
Has the issue where a guest component can cause the host to write arbitrary data to any location in memory been fixed in Wasmtime?
Has the issue causing the host to panic when compiling valid guest code with the `table.fill` instruction been fixed?
Has the issue with Wasmtime loading extra bytes from memory when compiling the f64x2.splat instruction without SSE3 been fixed?
Has the issue with Wasmtime panicking when lifting flags-typed values with ignored bits been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue with Wasmtime's string transcoding causing host panics from unaligned pointers been fixed in the latest version?
Has the issue where Wasmtime reads beyond a WebAssembly's memory bounds when transcoding UTF-16 strings been fixed?
Has the issue with cosign verify-blob-attestation incorrectly validating malformed payloads been fixed?
Can URL-encoded paths still bypass configuration name validation and allow reading files outside the intended directory?
Has the issue where users can read text files without download permission been fixed?
Has the issue where access rules for a directory also match unintended subdirectories been fixed?
Can links in the navigation template still execute JavaScript code when an administrator injects a javascript: URI into the href attribute?
Has the issue with the replace filter allowing attackers to bypass memory limits and cause out-of-memory conditions been fixed?
Has the issue where an app can access a USB device not matching the requested filters been fixed in our current Electron version?
Has the issue where aiohttp reads large multipart form fields into memory before checking size limits been fixed?
Has the issue where an attacker can inject extra headers by manipulating the content_type parameter in aiohttp been fixed?
Has the issue with the DNS cache growing indefinitely and using excessive memory when making requests to many hosts been fixed?
